dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 9637 IH: Protecting Columbia River Salmon Act of 2026
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Ms. Perez introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on Natural Resources

form.official-title: To amend the Marine Mammal Protection Act of 1972 to authorize intentional lethal take by certain Indian Tribes of California sea lions and Steller sea lions in a specified portion of the Columbia River, and for other purposes.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Protecting Columbia River Salmon Act of 2026.

section.header: Intentional lethal take by certain Indian Tribes of certain pinnipeds in Columbia River

subsection.text: Section 120 of the Marine Mammal Protection Act of 1972 (16 U.S.C. 1389) is amended by adding at the end the following:

subsection.header: Intentional lethal take by certain Indian Tribes of certain pinnipeds in Columbia River

paragraph.text: Notwithstanding any other provision of law, a covered Indian Tribe may intentionally lethally take covered pinnipeds on covered waters.
With respect to the intentional lethal take of covered pinnipeds on covered waters, a covered Indian Tribe—

subclause.text: the number of covered pinnipeds the covered Indian Tribe may take; or
when the covered Indian Tribe may take covered pinnipeds; and

clause.text: notwithstanding whether the covered pinnipeds are individually identifiable; and
in a manner the covered Indian Tribe determines to be humane; and
using methods the covered Indian Tribe determines appropriate.

paragraph.text: A covered Indian Tribe may designate a person to intentionally lethally take covered pinnipeds on covered waters in accordance with the requirements of paragraph (2) on behalf of the covered Indian Tribe.
Nothing in this subsection may be construed to limit existing Tribal rights or authorities under treaties, executive orders, or other provisions of law.

subparagraph.text: The term covered Indian Tribe means an Indian Tribe (as such term is defined in section 102 of the Federally Recognized Indian Tribe List Act of 1994 (25 U.S.C. 5130)) that has ancestral ties to any part of covered waters.
The term covered pinniped means a—

clause.text: Steller sea lion (Eumetopias jubatus) that is part of the Eastern Distinct Population Segment of Steller sea lions.

subparagraph.text: The term covered waters means the mainstem and tributaries of the Columbia River in the States of Washington and Oregon from River Mile 0 to the McNary Dam.
